quick_facts.label: Recruiting examination
quick_facts.value: For external candidates, the principal route is the Staff Selection Commission (SSC) Combined Graduate Level Examination (CGLE); SSC’s CGL notice lists Junior Statistical Officer (JSO) under the Ministry of Statistics & Programme Implementation (MoSPI).

quick_facts.label: Service and classification
quick_facts.value: JSO is listed by SSC as a Group B post in MoSPI. MoSPI’s Subordinate Statistical Service (SSS) documentation describes the corresponding Level 6 statistical-function post as Group B, non-gazetted and non-ministerial.

quick_facts.label: Eligibility snapshot
quick_facts.value: In the SSC CGL 2025 notice, JSO eligibility was either a bachelor’s degree plus at least 60% in Mathematics at Class 12 level, or a bachelor’s degree with Statistics as a degree-level subject. Eligibility rules and cut-off dates can change by examination cycle.

quick_facts.label: Specialist examination component
quick_facts.value: Applicants who apply for and are shortlisted for JSO take the Statistics Paper-II in Tier II in addition to the applicable Paper-I components; SSC prepares JSO merit from specified Tier-II components subject to qualifying requirements.

quick_facts.label: Posting scope
quick_facts.value: The SSS is a central cadre administered by MoSPI, with statistical posts in participating ministries, departments and organisations; service rules state that officers may be required to serve anywhere in India.

quick_facts.label: Pay context
quick_facts.value: The SSC CGL 2025 notice placed JSO in Pay Level 6, whose published pay-matrix range was ₹35,400–₹1,12,400. This is basic-pay context, not a guaranteed take-home salary: allowances, deductions, location and future pay revisions affect actual pay.

sections.heading: What a Junior Statistical Officer does
paragraphs: JSO is a statistical-function role within MoSPI’s Subordinate Statistical Service rather than a single, identical desk assignment. The exact work depends on the cadre unit and division to which the officer is posted. MoSPI’s official directory shows JSOs working in, among other places, Economic Statistics, Social Statistics, Infrastructure and Project Monitoring, Coordination and International Cooperation, training, and field-operations-related units.

At the organisational level, MoSPI’s statistical work covers collection, processing, validation, compilation and dissemination of official statistics. For example, the National Statistical Office states that its Field Operations Division conducts survey fieldwork, while the relevant survey division may prepare instruments, process data and prepare reports. A JSO should therefore expect work centred on reliable data and statistical outputs, with the balance between field operations, data handling, compilation, coordination and reporting varying by posting.
bullets: Work with statistical data and official-statistics processes in the assigned cadre unit.
Support survey or administrative-data workflows where the assigned unit is responsible for collection, processing, validation, compilation or dissemination.
Apply statistical concepts carefully and maintain documentation, data quality and confidentiality expectations appropriate to government statistical work.

sections.heading: Entry through SSC CGL
paragraphs: The direct-entry route is SSC CGL. In the SSC CGL 2025 notice, candidates had to indicate that they were applying for JSO and possessed its essential qualification. The notice set the JSO age band at 18–32 years as of its specified cut-off date; category-based relaxations and the relevant date must always be checked in the notice for the cycle being applied to.

JSO is distinct within CGL because shortlisted applicants take Tier-II Paper-II (Statistics). SSC states that Tier-I is used for category-wise shortlisting to Tier II, and that final JSO merit is based on the prescribed Tier-II aggregate, subject to qualifying the required Paper-I modules. Vacancies, dates, cut-offs, disability suitability and age treatment are cycle-specific, so an applicant should treat the live SSC notice as controlling.

For people already employed in recognised statistical feeder posts, SSS documentation also provides a limited internal promotion/absorption channel to the Level 6 statistical post. That is not a substitute for the external SSC CGL route for a new graduate.
bullets: Meet the JSO-specific academic condition in the applicable SSC CGL notice.
Apply for JSO specifically in the CGL application and submit post preferences when SSC calls for them.
Prepare for Tier I and the Tier-II Statistics paper, alongside Paper-I’s mathematical, reasoning, English, general-awareness, computer-knowledge and data-entry components as applicable.

sections.heading: Skills to build
paragraphs: Statistics is the core assessable domain: SSC reserves a 100-question, 200-mark Tier-II Paper-II in Statistics for shortlisted JSO applicants. The general Tier-II Paper-I scheme also includes mathematical abilities, reasoning, English, general awareness, computer knowledge and a data-entry task. These are examination requirements, but they are also a practical guide to the foundation skills needed to enter the role.

Beyond exam preparation, officers benefit from disciplined quantitative reasoning, careful handling of tabular data, documentation and clear communication. MoSPI’s survey operations describe data collection, processing, validation and final reporting as linked stages, so accuracy, consistency checking and an ability to work with defined procedures are especially relevant.
bullets: Probability, statistical inference, sampling, descriptive statistics and applied statistical reasoning.
Quantitative aptitude and accurate interpretation of tables, indicators and data outputs.
Computer literacy, precise data entry and comfort working with structured procedures.
Clear written communication and careful quality-control habits.

sections.heading: Workplace and mobility
paragraphs: Although SSC’s CGL listing identifies MoSPI as the ministry for JSO recruitment, the SSS is wider than a single headquarters office. Its cadre documentation lists Level 6 and Level 7 statistical posts across many participating central ministries, departments and organisations, while MoSPI remains the cadre-controlling authority.

A posting may be office-based in a statistical, policy, programme-monitoring or coordination division, or linked to field-survey operations. MoSPI’s directory includes JSOs in New Delhi divisions and its service rules make officers liable to serve anywhere in India. Candidates should therefore not assume that selection guarantees a particular city or a purely desk-based assignment.
bullets: Central-government cadre environment under MoSPI’s cadre control.
Potential assignments across participating ministries and statistical organisations.
All-India service liability under the applicable SSS rules.

sections.heading: Career progression
paragraphs: The established next grade in the SSS structure is Senior Statistical Officer (SSO), a Group B gazetted Level 7 post. MoSPI’s published SSS schedule states that Level 6 Statistical Officers are eligible for promotion to SSO after five years of regular service, through selection on the recommendation of the Departmental Promotion Committee. The terminology in SSS material has at times described the Level 6 post as Statistical Officer/erstwhile JSO, while SSC’s CGL notices continue to recruit it as JSO.

A MoSPI promotion order illustrates the path in practice: it records promotion of a JSO in Level 6 to SSO in Level 7, effective on assumption of charge. Actual timing depends on eligibility, vacancies, assessment by the competent promotion process and applicable rules; it should not be treated as automatic on completing a stated number of years.
bullets: Progression: promotion to Senior Statistical Officer / Level 7, subject to the governing rules and selection process.
Long-term development can include specialised experience across statistical divisions, survey operations, data governance, policy support and training assignments.

sections.heading: Compensation context
paragraphs: As published in SSC’s CGL 2025 notice, JSO was a Pay Level 6 post with a stated basic-pay matrix range of ₹35,400 to ₹1,12,400. This is the pay-level range, not a promise of a particular monthly in-hand amount.

Actual monthly compensation is variable. It depends on the officer’s basic-pay stage, admissible central-government allowances, place of posting, recoveries and any subsequent government pay decisions. Candidates should use the salary table in the notice for the relevant recruitment cycle and official pay/allowance orders rather than relying on coaching-site take-home estimates.
bullets: Published CGL 2025 basic-pay context: Pay Level 6, ₹35,400–₹1,12,400.
Promotion to the documented SSO grade is associated with Pay Level 7 in MoSPI’s SSS material.
Treat all salary figures as dated policy context rather than a fixed net-pay quotation.
